verses.1: A Psalm of David. Blessed be the LORD, my rock, Who trains my hands for war, And my fingers for battle;
verses.2: My faithfulness and my fortress, My stronghold and my savior, My shield and He in whom I take refuge, Who subdues my people under me.
verses.3: LORD, what is man, that You look after him? Or a son of man, that You think of him?
verses.4: Man is like the breath; His days are like a passing shadow.
verses.5: Bend down Your heavens, LORD, and come down; Touch the mountains, that they may smoke.
verses.6: Flash forth lightning and scatter them; Send out Your arrows and confuse them.
verses.7: Reach out with Your hand from on high; Rescue me and save me from great waters, From the hand of foreigners
verses.8: Whose mouths speak deceit, And whose right hand is a right hand of falsehood.
verses.9: God, I will sing a new song to You; On a harp of ten strings I will sing praises to You,
verses.10: Who gives salvation to kings, Who rescues His servant David from the evil sword.
verses.11: Rescue me and save me from the hand of foreigners, Whose mouth speaks deceit And whose right hand is a right hand of falsehood.
verses.12: When our sons in their youth are like growing plants, And our daughters like corner pillars fashioned for a palace,
verses.13: Our granaries are full, providing every kind of produce, And our flocks deliver thousands and ten thousands in our fields;
verses.14: May our cattle be bred Without mishap and without loss, May there be no outcry in our streets!
verses.15: Blessed are the people who are so situated; Blessed are the people whose God is the LORD!
